title: Transactional DDL in schema migration v6 is load-bearing
description: db.rs migration v6 wraps all ALTER TABLE statements in an explicit SQLite transaction (BEGIN → multiple DDL → PRAGMA user_version = 6 → COMMIT). This prevents partial migration: a crash mid-migration rolls back all column additions, leaving user_version < 6, so the next open retries cleanly. Without the transaction, a process kill leaves orphaned columns and makes every subsequent open fail with 'duplicate column name'. A contributor simplifying the migration code or copy-pasting a single-statement migration pattern will drop this transaction. Worth a comment specifically calling out 'do not inline the transaction, DDL is transactional in SQLite but only if you wrap it'.
close_reason: Already documented. db.rs lines 297–301 have a 4-line comment explaining that SQLite DDL is transactional, why the transaction is required, and what happens without it ('duplicate column name'). Nothing to add.
